title: Infinite Affection Points
description: Shimamura wakes up with the ability to see people's affection points. She tests it on her family, Yashiro, Nagafuji—and then Adachi. A playful, heartwarming exploration of love, numbers, and what they really mean.
content:
For no particular reason and without any warning, I suddenly gained the ability to see people's affection points. Weird stuff happens to me so often that going along with it has practically become routine. Probably.

Since I knew this weird ability would disappear in a day, I crawled out of bed without hesitation to try it out.

'Good morning. Come eat already,'

Mom called from the kitchen, gesturing for me to sit.

As soon as I took my seat, I saw my little sister beside me. Unlike me, who had just rolled out of bed looking like a disaster, she had already washed up and tied her hair neatly. She took after Mom in that way—always full of energy first thing in the morning.

Noticing me stare, she turned toward me with her glass of milk.

'Wh—what is it, sis?'

'Nothing… just thinking you look cute today too.'

'Huh?'

I'd thought maybe if I flattered her, her affection would go up, but all it raised was her suspicion.

'Mom, Sis is acting weird.'

'Hehehe~, she's always weird, isn't she?'

I really didn't want Mom to be the one teasing me—especially not with that carefree voice while she rests her chin on her hand.

On weekday mornings, it was always the same scene: my sister and I on one side, Mom and Yashiro on the other. Yashiro was wearing those lion-print pajamas again, happily spreading jam on her bread. She had been living in our house for a long time.

'Shimamura-san, do you want some jam too?'

'I do.'

Yeah—if her affection for me were any lower, she definitely wouldn't have passed me the jam. As for Yashiro's affection level… honestly, I have no idea what it's going to look like.

When breakfast ended and I got up from the table, Mom handed me a cloth-wrapped bundle.

'Your lunch.'

'Mm… hey, um… thanks. For everything.'

I tried to say something I normally would never say out loud. Mom blinked in surprise, then laughed it off.

'Hahaha, thank me even more!'

A casual laugh—then +3 popped up above her head. Ohh, so that's how it works.

When someone's affection changes, a little number pops up above them with a ding. A positive number means their affection went up. Unfortunately, I can't see the actual affection value—so for all I know, it might be going from -500 to -497.

Interesting—seeing her inner self exposed like this is actually kind of fun. But—once the novelty wears off, I have a feeling this whole thing is going to get boring fast.

While I was getting ready for school, I saw the 'lion' from earlier bouncing down the hallway—full of energy after breakfast as usual.

Ignoring the fact she could jump high enough to reach the top of my head, I figured raising her affection level would be easy.

So I decided to test it. I grabbed a piece of candy from the kitchen and gave it to her.

'Waaah~'

A number exploded above her head—so many digits I had to count the zeros one by one. One… two… eight? Yep. Eight zeros.

Seven hundred million affection points. From a single piece of candy.

She immediately chomped down and swallowed it. Then her affection dropped… probably around six hundred million.

So the net gain was around +100 million. Crazy.

'So eating the candy made your mood drop that fast, huh.'

'Fufufu~'

I tugged her cheeks, but she just smiled like always.

Okay. Enough of that.

What I really wanted to know was Adachi. Can I see her affection? How much it rises… and how fast…

I walked to school thinking about it, and soon spotted a familiar back.

I sped up a little. Red-framed glasses, cheerful vibe—yep. Nagafuji.

She turned when she heard my footsteps and smiled.

'Yo, Shima-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-ma~!'

'Say it again.'

'Heh, Shima-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-shi-ma~!'

Pretty sure the number of 'shi's changed between the two greetings.

We walked together—rare for just the two of us.

Nagafuji's affection, like Yashiro's, felt impossible to guess. Would it go up?

'What's wrong, Shima-shi~?'

Maybe I annoyed her, because only a single 'shi' remained on her nickname. And then she immediately followed up with:

'Yay~~~'

'Yay~'

She gave a thumbs-up, so I returned the gesture. She nodded, satisfied.

I glanced above her head—nothing. No increase. Her thumb drooped back down. Mine hurt a little. This was dumb. Whatever.

'You're not with Hino this morning?'

'We don't live in the same house, you know.'

She said it like it was obvious. Then—

'But who knows about the future.'

'Eh…'

I felt like she was hinting at something huge… then I remembered: since this was Nagafuji we're talking about, it probably meant she'd just move into Hino's house one day without asking.

'Why are you looking at me like that?'

'A Nagafuji without Hino just feels wrong.'

'Really~?'

She puffed her nose proudly, and a +2 floated above her head. Was that… for me?

She soon ran off to 'go find Hino,' not 'meet Hino.' That was very Nagafuji. I felt like my affection for her went up too.

Once I was alone again, I spotted Adachi at the school gate. She must have arrived early, realized I wasn't there yet, and waited for me. Adorable.

Because of her, I'd even picked up healthier habits—like trying to get to school earlier.

When our eyes met, her lips curved into the softest smile. When she wasn't trying, Adachi could smile so sweetly. And knowing I was the one who made her smile like that… I let out a tiny, smug 'hehe,' which immediately startled her.

As I walked toward her, her affection level started rising. I hadn't even done anything yet, but ding ding ding—numbers kept popping up nonstop. Mostly two-digit increases, sometimes three. Huh…

'Morning, Shimamura…'

Her smile stiffened into something awkward—pretty normal for her. But her affection kept climbing anyway. What on earth was causing that?

I glanced down at myself out of reflex. Everything looked normal… except she couldn't see my face from that angle.

'Shimamura…?'

'Ah—mm. Morning.'

I returned a small smile. Her affection spiked again.

'…Ugh.'

Whether we walked into school, changed our shoes, or headed to class—her affection just kept skyrocketing.

So… as long as I exist, anything I do automatically boosts her affection? Isn't Adachi spoiling me a bit too much?

Her affection rises so easily it makes anything I do feel pointless. Not good. But I don't have a clue how to stop it.

And I can't exactly tell her, 'Hey, by the way, I can see your feelings.'

I sat at my seat. Even taking out my textbook triggered another affection rise somewhere behind me.

Thinking about this happening every day made my cheeks burn. I could feel her gaze on me. When I stole a glance back, our eyes met—Adachi flinched, then held my gaze with shy determination. I waved at her lightly. A giant number popped up. Should I feel proud… or worried for her? I faced forward and pinched my cheeks.

Adachi really does like my face, doesn't she…? Maybe I should have more confidence in that. But next to someone as stunning as Adachi, any confidence I have disappears instantly. Actually… maybe I should be confident—my sense of taste is probably tuned to Adachi's beauty. To me, she's more beautiful than any so-called 'absolute beauty' I've ever seen.

Adachi is the best.

I think my affection for her just shot up 300 points. My face felt warm.

During class, the sound of affection notifications mingled with the teacher's voice and chalk scraping the board. Adachi, pay attention… I can't believe her grades were better than mine.

Would her affection drop if I annoyed her? Part of me wanted to know—but I didn't actually want it to happen.

Normally, to test that, you'd have to do something awful, right? Shout at her. Slap her shoulder suddenly… Could I? To someone as perfect as Adachi?

'No way…' I ended up giving myself a mental thumbs-up, like some ridiculous American meme.

So… Adachi's affection can only go up?

Maybe I didn't need to go extreme. Something harmless—like circling her like a mosquito during break…

'Shi–Shimamura?'

'Yaaa~!'

I 'bumped' into her. Well… more like lightly nudging her shoulder. She bent her knees and whimpered, like plastic melting in summer heat.

Even then, her affection kept climbing.

We stared at each other. Her face went red, and she looked down. Affection +???

… Just how? This is completely beyond me.

I pulled out my trump card—reaching for her face. I pinched her cheeks gently, just enough to tease, but not painful. Adachi froze, like, 'Huhhh?' trembling a little.

'Kehehehe.'

I grinned meaninglessly while stretching her cheeks. She made weird noises but smiled awkwardly along.

Even confused, she went along with me. And again: ding ding ding.

Physical contact? That must be it—ding ding ding ding.

'…Hah.'

I gave up. No matter how hard I tried, I couldn't think of anything 'bad' enough to make her affection go down. And honestly, if it ever did drop, I think I'd be devastated.

So… better to just let it rise. Her love didn't seem to have a ceiling anyway. It kept climbing higher and higher, endless and bright, like a swirling galaxy. If Adachi was her own little universe of love, then maybe I needed to raise my own standards too.

I pictured numbers popping into existence—always in neat little three-digit chunks—and completely stopped paying attention in class.

Well, whatever. This was fine too.

After school, as usual: 'Um… are you free today?' Adachi asked, shyly approaching me.

Of course I was. I took the hand she offered.

'I was thinking we could get donuts today.'

'Eh—yeah.'

Has Adachi ever turned me down before? I couldn't remember.

'Adachi… if there's something you don't want to do, you should say no.'

'…W-what do you mean…?'

Ding ding ding.

No way—there's no reason that should've earned that many points. My face heated up… Maybe I should try to become someone actually worthy of all this affection.

At the donut shop by the station, I picked out two donuts for each of us, plus a few for the sisters.

'Today's on me. Please bag these,' I said as I headed to the register. Adachi's eyes went wide.

'D-did something good happen…?'

'Eh? Not really.'

'Then I'll pay—'

'It's fine, it's fine.'

I stopped her hand and stepped ahead.

After paying, I looked at her.

'Thanks…' She said shyly, a warm little smile on her lips. But her affection number barely budged.

No good? I had hoped for something massive—maybe not 700 million like Yashiro, but at least seven million. Adachi wasn't really into food, I suppose. Buying snacks didn't impress her.

Her interests? There's only one. Me. Embarrassing, but true.

Donuts weren't going to do it. Oh well.

Looking for seats, I glanced around the station. There's hardly anyone around.

'Ah, got it,' I said, setting down the tray and turning to her.

'Let's go on a date, Adachi.'

I smiled softly and held up one finger, showing I meant it.

Adachi froze mid-movement, her shoulders trembling, almost dropping the tray.

'He… hehehe…'

Her relaxed expression probably mirrored mine. Ding ding. Her affection rose again, though not dramatically. Even for Adachi, four digits seemed hard to reach. What does four-digit affection even feel like?

I wondered how many digits my affection for Adachi had. The ceiling above the donut shop gave no answers.

'For example, Adachi,' I asked as we ate, savoring the sugary sweetness, 'would you want to see how much affection I have for you? Like… as a number?'

'Numbers?' She blinked. Adachi didn't really get games or anything like that, so it wasn't clicking for her.

'Mhm. Like a number that shows how much I like you.'

'I w—' She started to answer, then froze. She slowly lowered her donut, humming to herself as she thought it over.

Watching her take it so seriously made me slow down too—though I didn't stop eating. The donuts were too good for that.

She kept thinking. Meanwhile, her affection kept rising on its own even though I wasn't doing anything. Honestly, amazing.

'…I want to know… but I'm also scared to know.'

'I see.'

For me, it would be easy. Even if someone disliked me, I'd just shrug it off. Seeing it as a number would actually be convenient.

'But for you…' I said, 'knowing the exact number might change how things feel.'

She nodded a little. 'I like not knowing. That uncertainty… it keeps me trying. If I knew the exact number—and if it ever went down—I'd be terrified. I don't want to see proof that your feelings for me could fade.'

………………

Ah. I get it. I really do.

That desperate sincerity of hers—that's exactly what brought me this far.

Hearing her say she didn't want it to drop.

Something tightened in my chest.

This ability… maybe I didn't need it anymore. Maybe it could just fade away now.

Ding.

'Eh?'

'That conversation just raised your affection by fifty points,' I said.

Even without relying on this power, making her affection rise was unbelievably easy.

Adachi murmured, 'Fifty…' letting the number linger on her tongue. Then it clicked, and her face flushed red as she lifted her donut like a shield. A small, gentle smile bloomed—soft and sweet, just like the pastry she was holding.

Ding ding.

'Since it's a date with Shimamura… my affection went up about a hundred twenty-seven points.'

'…What?'

Too cute. Too honest. I ended up laughing out loud.

Was she doing this intentionally? Or was this just naturally who she was?

At this point I was seriously wondering if Adachi was some kind of love goddess.

And just when I thought she couldn't surprise me more—the number she said matched exactly the one floating above her head.

Then—'Here. A gift for you.'

'Yay~!'

Fifteen billion.

Her affection shot from fifteen to fifty billion in a heartbeat.

Then, after she finished eating, it calmly dropped by forty-nine billion again.

Those ridiculous, over-the-top fluctuations somehow made me genuinely happy.

Wow.

Just… wow
